repo_notes.content: RLix is a Ray-based control plane for concurrent LLM reinforcement learning on shared GPUs. Entry point is rlix.init(create_if_missing=True) in rlix/init.py. Core modules: rlix/orchestrator/, rlix/scheduler/, rlix/pipeline/, rlix/protocol/. ROLL provides the training and inference workers; RLix manages GPU allocation and pipeline lifecycle. Design principles: fail fast, no fault tolerance, no backward compatibility, happy-path only.

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce RLix, explain its purpose as a Ray-based control plane for concurrent LLM RL training with GPU time-sharing, and provide a high-level architectural summary

page_notes.content: RLix is control-plane only; ROLL provides the workers. The core insight: RL pipelines alternate training and rollout, leaving GPUs idle. RLix fills those gaps by running multiple pipelines concurrently with two-level sharing: cross-pipeline elastic resize and within-pipeline multi-LoRA.

pages.title: Architecture at a Glance
pages.purpose: Present the high-level system architecture showing Control Plane, Pipelines, GPU Clusters, and their relationships

page_notes.content: Three singleton Ray actors (orchestrator, scheduler, resource_manager) plus one coordinator actor per pipeline. See rlix/orchestrator/, rlix/scheduler/, rlix/pipeline/coordinator.py.

pages.title: Key Concepts
pages.purpose: Define fundamental terminology: pipelines, coordinators, GPU time-sharing, priority-based scheduling, LoRA adapters, rollout workers, and scheduling stages

page_notes.content: Key types are in rlix/protocol/types.py: Priority enum (7 tiers, only GENERATION is preemptable), ActionResponse, ProgressReport. Cluster types: actor_train, actor_infer, critic, reference, reward. Only actor_infer clusters are elastically resized.

pages.title: Getting Started
pages.purpose: Guide users through initial setup, running their first pipeline, and understanding basic configuration

page_notes.content: See README.md quick-start section and examples/start_multi_pipeline_test.py.

pages.title: Installation and Setup
pages.purpose: Cover dependencies, Ray cluster setup, environment variables, and initial configuration requirements

page_notes.content: Required deps: tg4perfetto>=0.0.6, protobuf<3.21.0, roll from GitHub. Optional env var RLIX_REQUIRED_GPUS_PER_NODE enforces a strict GPU-per-node topology at init.

pages.title: Quick Start Example
pages.purpose: Walk through running start_multi_pipeline_test.py, explain the initialization sequence, and show expected output

page_notes.content: See examples/start_multi_pipeline_test.py. Supports single pipeline, two full-finetune pipelines, or full-finetune plus multi-LoRA. Init sequence is in README.md.

pages.title: Configuration Basics
pages.purpose: Introduce Hydra configuration files, explain cluster_tp_configs and cluster_device_mappings, and show basic pipeline config structure

page_notes.content: Config examples in examples/rlix_test/. Required keys: cluster_tp_configs and cluster_device_mappings. Multi-LoRA requires megatron_train strategy and is_lora_optimizer_isolated=true. train_device_mapping must be a subset of infer_device_mapping.

pages.title: Control Plane
pages.purpose: Document the singleton control plane components (Orchestrator, Scheduler, ResourceManager) that manage all pipelines

page_notes.content: Three singleton actors created by rlix.init(). Actor names, the RLIX_NAMESPACE constant, and protocol types (Priority, ActionResponse, ProgressReport) are all defined in rlix/protocol/types.py.

pages.title: Orchestrator
pages.purpose: Explain Orchestrator's role in pipeline lifecycle management: allocate_pipeline_id, register_pipeline, admit_pipeline, kill_pipeline, shutdown

page_notes.content: Source: rlix/orchestrator/orchestrator.py. Owns the four-step pipeline lifecycle: allocate_pipeline_id, register_pipeline (validates topology via rlix/scheduler/validation.py), admit_pipeline (creates coordinator actor), kill_pipeline (tears down coordinator and releases scheduler state).

pages.title: Scheduler
pages.purpose: Document SchedulerImpl's priority-based GPU allocation, request/release APIs, pending request queuing, and central scheduling loop

page_notes.content: Source: rlix/scheduler/scheduler.py (SchedulerImpl). Core APIs: request_gpus (async generator), notify_ready_to_release (blocks until shrink committed), report_progress. Internal state in rlix/scheduler/state.py; plan types in rlix/scheduler/types.py.

pages.title: Resource Manager
pages.purpose: Explain ResourceManager's role in GPU topology validation, cluster resource snapshots, and gpus-per-node enforcement

page_notes.content: Source: rlix/scheduler/resource_manager.py. Validates GPU topology at startup and provides cluster snapshots. init_topology enforces consistent gpus-per-node across all alive Ray nodes.

pages.title: Pipeline Coordination
pages.purpose: Document the PipelineCoordinator actor's role in managing individual pipelines and coordinating with the control plane

page_notes.content: Source: rlix/pipeline/coordinator.py. Protocol defined in rlix/protocol/coordinator.py. Key design: coordinator calls request_gpus and notify_ready_to_release on behalf of the pipeline so ROLL training code is fully decoupled from GPU scheduling (recipe-transparent scheduling). resize_infer and sync_lora_weights are serialized via _resize_sync_lock.

pages.title: Pipeline Actor Creation
pages.purpose: Detail how PipelineCoordinator determines pipeline type, injects environment variables, and creates the pipeline actor

page_notes.content: Selects RollFullFinetunePipeline or RollMultiLoraPipeline based on model_args.adapters. Validates: reward cluster must be CPU-only, sleep_level=2 required, offload_nccl=True required. Injects PIPELINE_ID, RLIX_CONTROL_PLANE, and thread-limiting env vars into ROLL runtime_env. See rlix/pipeline/coordinator.py for the full env var list.

pages.title: Pipeline Implementations
pages.purpose: Document the two pipeline implementations: RollFullFinetunePipeline for full finetuning and RollMultiLoraPipeline for multi-LoRA training

page_notes.content: Sources: rlix/pipeline/full_finetune_pipeline.py and rlix/pipeline/multi_lora_pipeline.py. Full-finetune wraps ROLL AgenticPipeline. Multi-LoRA wraps AgenticMultiLoraPipeline and requires megatron_train strategy and isolated optimizers per adapter.

pages.title: Training Loop Phases
pages.purpose: Detail the sequential phases in the training loop: expand, rollout, shrink, old log probs, advantage, training, sync

page_notes.content: Seven Priority tiers map to phases. GENERATION(6) is the only preemptable phase. For multi-LoRA correctness: adapter must be disabled during REF_LOG_PROBS and enabled during OLD_LOG_PROBS. No GAE critic in multi-LoRA mode.

pages.title: GPU Resource Management
pages.purpose: Explain how RLix achieves GPU time-sharing through priority-based scheduling, elastic workers, and background rebalancing

page_notes.content: Core files: rlix/scheduler/scheduler.py, rlix/scheduler/state.py, rlix/scheduler/types.py. Scheduler rebalances continuously using ProgressReport demand signals. Two-level sharing: cross-pipeline via resize_infer(), within-pipeline multi-LoRA via sync_lora_weights().

pages.title: Scheduling Priorities and Allocation
pages.purpose: Document the seven priority levels (0-6), which stages are preemptable, and how priority determines GPU allocation strategy (fixed vs gap-ratio)

page_notes.content: Priority enum in rlix/protocol/types.py (INITIALIZATION=0 through GENERATION=6). Non-GENERATION priorities get fixed device_mapping GPUs. GENERATION uses gap-ratio planning proportional to trajectory demand. ExecutionPlan in rlix/scheduler/types.py encodes the resolved ops.

pages.title: Elastic Rollout Workers
pages.purpose: Explain how actor_infer workers expand onto idle GPUs and shrink when higher-priority stages need capacity

page_notes.content: Expand adds DP ranks via ROLL resize_infer. Shrink is two-phase: stop routing, then sleep_level=2 drops model weights from VRAM. sleep_level=2 is required (not sleep_level=1) and is validated in PipelineCoordinator.

pages.title: Model Synchronization
pages.purpose: Document the selective weight sync mechanism for pushing trained weights to inference workers

page_notes.content: Only active when RLIX_CONTROL_PLANE env var is set. After each training tick, ROLL builds a versioned CPU bucket cache and replays it to vLLM workers via IPC (colocated) or NCCL broadcast (remote). Triggered from rlix side by coordinator sync_lora_weights().
